ocr.text: The table lists various plasmids along with their references and notes. The plasmid pL-RPB1 is referenced as Ref. 9 and has no
additional notes. The plasmid pRS315, referenced as Ref. 14, is noted to be referred to as pLEU in the text. The plasmid pVS31,
associated with Ref. 11, is described as a plasmid for making the pif1-m2 mutation. The plasmid pJMD2, with reference Ref.
13, is identified as a plasmid for recursive directional ligation used to create CTD mutants. The plasmid pSMF2, attributed to
this work, is characterized as pL-RPB1 with XmaI and SacI sites flanking the CTD coding region and is referred to as pRPB1 in
the text. The plasmid pRPB1-CTD8, also from this work, is derived from pSMF2 and contains 8 CTD repeats. The pRPB1-
CTD10 plasmid, another product of this work, includes 10 CTD repeats, while the plasmid pRPB1-CTD14 features 14 CTD
repeats, both stemming from pSMF2. The plasmid pRPB1-CTD26, also from this work, is noted for having 26 CTD repeats.
Finally, the plasmid pRPB1–4stop, attributed to this work, is described as pSMF2 with repeats 8–10 interrupted by 4 repeats
containing stop codons. Each plasmid's unique designation and functionality are clearly articulated in the context of their
respective references and notes, establishing their importance in the research presented.
Plasmid  Reference  Notes
pL-RPB1  Ref. 9
pRS315  Ref. 14  Referred to as pLEU in the text
pVS31  Ref. 11  Plasmid for making pif1-m2 mutation
pJMD2  Ref. 13  Plasmid for recursive directional ligation used to make CTD mutants
pSMF2  This work  pL-RPB1 with XmaI and SacI sites flanking the CTD coding region. Referred to as pRPB1 in the text
pRPB1-CTD8  This work  pSMF2 with 8 CTD repeats
pRPB1-CTD10  This work  pSMF2 with 10 CTD repeats
pRPB1-CTD14  This work  pSMF2 with 14 CTD repeats
pRPB1-CTD26  This work  pSMF2 with 26 CTD repeats
pRPB1–4stop  This work  pSMF2 with repeats 8–10 interrupted with 4 repeats containing stop codons
